channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Extends Cease-Fire With Iran
item.description: The pause in hostilities had been set to expire within hours. The president said Pakistan, which is trying to mediate an end to the war, requested he hold off any attacks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: How the Cole Disaster Drove the U.S. to Develop New Warship Defenses
item.description: The Navy destroyers enforcing a blockade of Iranian ports carry weapons fielded after an American warship was attacked and nearly sunk more than 25 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish, John Ismay and Constant Méheut
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The damaged destroyer Cole being taken out of the Port of Aden by tugboats after it was attacked in October 2000.

item.title: Trump Is Said to Be in Talks to Send Afghans Who Aided U.S. Forces to Congo
item.description: A U.S. aid worker said that the Afghans, who were evacuated to Qatar, would face a choice between moving to the Democratic Republic of Congo and living under the Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Megha Rajagopalan, Eileen Sullivan and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:credit: Sgt. Jimmie Baker/U.S. Army, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A photograph released by the U.S. Army showing Afghans being processed at Camp As Sayliyah, in Qatar, in August 2021.

item.title: Fired U.K. Official Describes ‘Pressure’ to Expedite Envoy’s Appointment
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s office wanted Peter Mandelson, a Jeffrey Epstein associate, swiftly installed as ambassador to Washington, the official said.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: The British prime minister, Keir Starmer, said he was not told about a security vetting team’s concerns about Peter Mandelson, the former ambassador to the United States.

item.title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
item.description: Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U.S. sites there recently.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim, Falih Hassan, Edward Wong and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Members of Kataib Hezbollah, an Iran-backed Iraqi militia, during a mourning procession in Baghdad this month for one of their comrades who was killed.

item.title: 2 Americans Killed in Mexico Crash Were C.I.A. Members
item.description: The two Americans were killed on Sunday when their vehicle crashed while returning from a counter-cartel operation led by Mexico’s armed forces in the state of Chihuahua.
item.dc:creator: Maria Abi-Habib, Dustin Volz, Adam Goldman and Paulina Villegas
item.media:description: A suspected methamphetamine processing lab in the northern Mexican state of Chihuahua.

item.title: Two Palestinians Killed in Israeli Shooting Near West Bank School
item.description: A child was among the dead, according to the Palestinian health ministry. The Israeli military said it had attempted to break up clashes in Al Mughayir, which has seen a surge of Israeli settler violence.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.media:description: The bodies of two Palestinians killed in the West Bank village of Al Mughayir, at a hospital in Ramallah on Tuesday.

item.title: Israeli Soldiers Sentenced to Jail for Damaging Jesus Statue in Lebanon
item.description: A statue of the crucifixion of Christ has been replaced after one soldier photographed another swinging what looked like a sledgehammer at its head.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

item.title: Teotihuacán Pyramids Shooting: Gunman Carried Notes on U.S. Mass Shootings
item.description: The man who killed one tourist at a Mexican pyramid and wounded several others had materials in his backpack tied to a 1999 U.S. attack, a possible reference to the Columbine High School massacre.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and David Shortell
item.media:description: Members of the Mexican National Guard at the perimeter of the Teotihuacán pyramids after the shooting.

item.title: London Tube Drivers’ Strike Halts a Key Line
item.description: The dispute over working conditions and hours was expected to cause widespread delays and service outages.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport Workers (RMT)
item.media:description: A sign warning travelers of strike-related delays at the St. James’s Park Underground station in London on Tuesday.

item.title: Japan to Sell More Weapons Abroad, Breaking With Postwar Pacifism
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reversed limits on arms exports as Japan faces rising threats from China and unpredictability from its main ally, the United States.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: A Japanese marine unit takes part in exercises in Tokunoshima, Japan, in 2023.

item.title: Nigeria Charges Six With Treason Over Coup Plot
item.description: The group, made up of civilians and former members of the country’s armed forces, is accused of treason linked to a failed coup attempt in October.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
Economic Community of West African States (Ecowas)
item.media:description: President Bola Tinubu of Nigeria at his swearing-in ceremony in Abuja in 2023.

item.title: Imprisoned for 30 Years, He Wooed Her With Love Poems
item.description: After a forced confession and a death sentence, a Kurdish poet spent 30 years in jail, where he discovered his voice. His literary champion, to everyone’s surprise, became his life partner.
item.dc:creator: Silvana Paternostro
item.media:credit: Bradley Secker for The New York Times
item.media:description: The poet Ilhan Sami Çomak, right, and his partner, Ipek Ozel, left, at home in Istanbul.

item.title: Europe Wanted a Say on the Iran War, but It’s Still on the Sidelines
item.description: A British-French plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz would give the continent a role. But Tehran and Washington are still calling the shots.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, President Emmanuel Macron of France, Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy at a conference in Paris on Friday.

item.title: Pope Leo Is Skipping Some of Africa’s Biggest Catholic Nations
item.description: As the pontiff arrives in the tiny authoritarian nation of Equatorial Guinea, some Catholics in Africa say they are excited about his visit but are feeling a little left out.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Friday.

item.title: A Year After Terrorist Attack, a Kashmir Town Longs for Tourists
item.description: Pahalgam, with steep ravines, grassy hillsides and pine forests, is a base for Hindu pilgrimages that business operators hope will revive visitors.
item.dc:creator: Showkat Nanda
item.media:credit: Showkat Nanda for The New York Times
item.media:description: Security officers are a constant presence on tourist routes in Pahalgam after last year’s attack.

item.title: U.S. Sends Plane to Cuba to Get Child in Transgender Custody Case
item.description: The F.B.I. believed the child was kidnapped by a transgender parent to potentially undergo gender transition surgery, according to court filings.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Frances Robles
item.media:description: A flight tracker showed that a U.S. government plane landed at the Jose Marti International Airport in Havana. It appeared to be part of an F.B.I. mission to retrieve an American child.

item.title: What Was the 2015 Iran Nuclear Deal?
item.description: President Trump has ridiculed President Barack Obama and the agreement, which he withdrew from during his first term. His critics say he could have avoided a war had he left it in place.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: Negotiators from the United States, Iran and European countries meeting to discuss a nuclear deal in Vienna in 2015.

item.title: Proposed Lifetime Smoking Ban to Become Law in Britain
item.description: The proposal, which was approved by Parliament on Tuesday, will ban the supply or sale of tobacco products to anyone born in 2009 or after, permanently.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: The proposed lifetime smoking ban will apply in England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland.

item.title: What I Saw Crossing Into South Lebanon
item.description: Our visual journalist David Guttenfelder traveled with displaced people returning to their homes in southern Lebanon, as a cease-fire paused the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder, Coleman Lowndes, Christina Thornell, Sutton Raphael, Joey Sendaydiego and Thomas Vollkommer

item.title: Trump Extends Cease-Fire, Even as Peace Talks Are Put on Hold
item.description: With the cease-fire on the verge of ending, President Trump said on Tuesday that he would keep it in place until Iran’s “proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other.”
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan, Maggie Haberman, Elian Peltier and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran meant to show that Iran has a tight grip on the Strait of Hormuz. The status of the strait is one of the sticking points in negotiations between Iran and the United States.

item.title: War Shock in Asia
item.description: The damage to a region that has driven global economic growth for decades may be hard to contain.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times
item.media:description: A floating fuel station in the Mekong River Delta, Vietnam.

item.title: Lyrids Meteor Shower: How to Watch, Peak Time and Weather Forecast
item.description: Active since last week, the Lyrids meteor shower is expected to reach peak viewing tonight.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller
item.media:description: The Lyrids meteor shower over Burg on the Baltic Sea island of Fehmarn off Germany in 2018.

item.title: Why Negotiations With Iran Might Take a While
item.description: The Iranians are notoriously slow negotiators. Our reporter David E. Sanger explains what the history of U.S.-Iran negotiations could mean for the latest round of talks.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Nikolay Nikolov, Paul Abowd, Rafaela Balster and Jordan Gantz

item.title: For Earth Day, a Few Signs of Hope for Our Planet
item.description: In a year of grim climate and environment news, we’ve compiled several hopeful signs about our planet’s future.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles
item.category: Supreme Court of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: The view of home from the other side of the moon.

item.title: Lebanese Bury Their Dead Amid a Lull in Fighting
item.description: Mass funerals for Hezbollah fighters and civilians were happening across southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mourners carrying coffins draped in Hezbollah flags in southern Lebanon on Tuesday.

item.title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
item.description: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance during peace talks with Iran in Islamabad earlier this month.

item.title: Trump Extends Iran Cease-Fire Before Deadline Expires; Vance’s Trip Is on Hold
item.description: President Trump attributed his decision to divisions among the Iranians about how to proceed and said he wanted to give them more time.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan, Maggie Haberman and Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Islamabad, Pakistan, this month during a previous round of negotiations between the United States and Iran.

item.title: Crossing the Divide
item.description: What one image tells us about the road back to southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.S. Military Stops and Boards M/T Tifani, Tanker Carrying Iranian Oil, Pentagon Says
item.description: The seizure was the latest U.S. effort to squeeze Iran’s oil-reliant economy, and came days after the Navy boarded an Iranian cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A photo released by the Defense Department appears to show the M/T Tifani.

item.title: ‘Donnyland’? Ukraine Proposes Naming Part of the Donbas in Trump’s Honor.
item.description: The proposal reflects a global reality in which governments appeal to President Trump’s vanity to get American might on their side.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Lynsey Addario for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sloviansk, in the industrial Donbas region, which was first invaded by Russia in 2014.

item.title: 2 Cruise Ships Clear Strait of Hormuz After Being Stranded for Weeks
item.description: The ships carried skeleton crews and no passengers, the cruise company said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A Mein Schiff cruise ship docked in Doha, Qatar, earlier this month.

item.title: Xi Calls for Hormuz to Reopen as China Balances Its Gulf Interests
item.description: The comments on the Strait of Hormuz by China’s leader, Xi Jinping, reflect Beijing’s complex relations with Iran and other Persian Gulf nations.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Berry Wang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, attending a meeting with the crown prince of Abu Dhabi in Beijing last week.

item.title: New Zealand Declares Emergency in Capital After It Was Battered by Torrential Rain
item.description: There were landslides, flash floods and the closure of more than 100 schools in Wellington and the surrounding region.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
item.media:description: Workers clearing debris from a damaged bridge on Tuesday after torrential rain in South Karori in Wellington, New Zealand.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Jonathan Swan, Farnaz Fassihi and Somini Sengupta

item.title: Iran’s Nonnuclear Deterrent
item.description: Disrupting the Strait of Hormuz turned out to be a powerful weapon. It might be a new way for Iran to keep adversaries at bay, even without a nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Facing Hard-Liners at Home and Bluster From Trump, Iran Sends Mixed Signals on Peace Talks
item.description: Publicly, Iranian officials refuse to confirm a second round of negotiations with the United States, even as they privately say they are making plans to attend.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman walking by a mural showing Iranian missiles attacking a U.S. ship, in Tehran in April.

item.title: Canadian Tourist Is Killed in Shooting at Mexico’s Pyramids
item.description: A gunman opened fire at the Teotihuacán archaeological site outside Mexico City before killing himself, the authorities said. Six Americans were among those injured, including two from gunshots.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Cyntia Barrera Díaz
item.media:description: Police officers at the Pyramid of the Moon at the Teotihuacán pyramids in Mexico after a deadly shooting on Monday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Talks Appear on Track to Resume Despite Mixed Messages
item.description: With the two-week cease-fire almost over, Vice President JD Vance was expected to head to Pakistan on Tuesday for the second round of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Farnaz Fassihi, Tyler Pager and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Islamabad, Pakistan, this month during a previous round of negotiations between the United States and Iran.

item.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
item.description: The container ship, the Touska, was sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury over links to Iranian weapons programs.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A screenshot of a video posted by U.S. Central Command on Sunday that was described as showing a U.S. Navy destroyer near an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Arabian Sea.

item.title: Mexico to Investigate Security Role of 2 U.S. Officials Killed in Crash
item.description: President Claudia Sheinbaum said the inquiry would focus on whether the involvement of the Americans in a drug-fighting operation violated Mexico’s national security laws and sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:description: A suspected methamphetamine processing lab in the northern Mexican state of Chihuahua.

item.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
item.description: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, last week.

item.title: Elon Musk Ignores French Prosecutors, Widening Tech Rift With Europe
item.description: Prosecutors investigating his social media company, X, had summoned him for a meeting. His no-show reflected a broader dispute over regulation.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter
item.media:description: Elon Musk speaking in Switzerland in January. He has previously denounced the French investigation.

item.title: In Lebanon, residents of the south make their way home over signs of war.
item.description: Some of the million people displaced by the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon are flooding back home since a 10-day truce between Israel and Lebanon took hold.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder
item.media:description: A Lebanese boy is welcomed by his aunt and uncle after crossing a destroyed bridge with other family members, in the background. They were crossing over the Litani River in the south of Lebanon near the village of Tayr Felsay, on Monday.

item.title: Israeli Soldier in Lebanon Sledgehammered a Statue of Jesus
item.description: The military is investigating the soldier. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed regret for any hurt caused to “believers in Lebanon and around the world.”
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

item.title: How Bruce the Parrot Landed Atop the Pecking Order, Without a Beak
item.description: The kea gained fame for learning to use a pebble to groom himself. Scientists were astounded by his next innovation.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer

item.title: A Decisive Win Raises Bulgarians’ Hopes for Change
item.description: An election victory by a former president, Rumen Radev, gives the country a chance for stability and to clean out the corruption that has stymied its growth.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.media:description: Rumen Radev, Bulgaria’s former president, at a polling site in Sofia, Bulgaria, on Sunday. His coalition, Progressive Bulgaria, secured a majority of seats in Parliament in Sunday’s election.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Is at a Standstill Again
item.description: Few vessels are crossing after Iran reversed course on reopening the vital waterway and ships came under attack.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships and boats in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Monday.

item.title: Starmer Voices Fury Over Being Kept in the Dark on Mandelson Vetting
item.description: During intense grilling in Parliament, Prime Minister Keir Starmer blamed civil servants for not telling him that Peter Mandelson had been rejected for top security clearances.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: A photograph released by the UK Parliament showing Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at the House of Commons on Monday.

item.title: A Leaner Saudi Arabia Turns From Grandiose Plans to Pragmatism
item.description: A decade after Mohammed bin Salman unveiled his “Vision 2030” program to transform the country’s economy, the kingdom is facing financial strains and reassessing its trajectory.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Saudi crown prince, Mohammed bin Salman, in Washington last fall. The prince’s plans and ambitions have grown more rapidly than the state’s financial capacity.

item.title: Ukraine, Short on Troops, Is Turning to Robots to Help Its War Efforts
item.description: Ukraine is using unmanned ground vehicles armed with bombs, guns or rockets to carry out attacks and keep its soldiers out of harm’s way.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.media:description: Ukrainian soldiers training with an unmanned ground vehicle in the Donbas region of eastern Ukraine last year. The country is eager to highlight its advances in technological warfare.

item.title: Pakistan’s Leaders Try to Contain Rising Anger Over Iran War at Home
item.description: With deep spiritual ties to Iran, Pakistan’s minority Shiites are angry about the killing of Iran’s top clerics in U.S.-Israeli strikes, complicating Pakistan’s role as mediator.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman and Asim Hafeez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: Javier Milei Tamed Argentina’s Inflation. Now He Wants to Reshape Its Values.
item.description: Argentina’s right-wing president has tamed the country’s runaway inflation. Now he wants to transform its values.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and Sarah Pabst
item.media:description: Javier Milei in Mar del Plata, Argentina, in January at the Derecha Fest, a large right-wing gathering.

item.title: Tsunami Warnings Downgraded in Japan After Strong Offshore Earthquake
item.description: The 7.7-magnitude undersea quake occurred off Iwate Prefecture, on the northeastern coast of Japan’s main island, Honshu.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.media:description: A tsunami warning is displayed on a television in Japan after a strong earthquake off the country’s northern coast on Monday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Farnaz Fassihi and Max Bearak

item.title: The Iran War Sent Shock Waves Through Asia That Are Likely to Spread
item.description: The Asia-Pacific was hit hard and quick by the war in Iran and its energy bottlenecks. Scenes of crisis there indicate that problems are multiplying and spreading.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times
item.media:description: A floating fuel station selling diesel last month in the Mekong River delta. Vietnam has been hit hard by rising fuel costs because of the war in Iran.

item.title: In Turkey, Middle Powers Ponder Diplomacy With a Rogue U.S.
item.description: The U.S. remains an essential player. The problem, one analyst said, is how to deal effectively with a power that is “indispensable, coercive and unpredictable at the same time.”
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey in Antalya on Friday, where he said the global system was in “a moral and existential crisis.”

item.title: 2 U.S. Government Workers Among 4 Killed in Car Crash in Mexico
item.description: Two of the victims were American and two were Mexican, the authorities in Chihuahua said. The accident took place on a treacherous mountain road.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Attorney General'S Office of Chihuahua, via, via Reuters
item.media:description: A suspected methamphetamine processing lab discovered in northern Mexico’s Chihuahua state.
